$schema: Library reference database for Design Intelligence MCP

libraries.name: shadcn/ui
libraries.description: Copy-paste components built on Radix UI + Tailwind CSS. Highly customizable, no dependency lock-in.
libraries.when_to_use: Default choice for React apps needing accessible, production-ready components with full design control. Use when you want to own the code and customize deeply.
libraries.example:
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'

<Button variant="default" size="lg">Click me</Button>

libraries.name: Radix UI
libraries.description: Unstyled, accessible UI primitives. Full design control with WAI-ARIA compliance baked in.
libraries.when_to_use: When you need full styling control with guaranteed accessibility. Foundation for custom design systems. Use when shadcn/ui components need deeper customization.

libraries.name: Mantine
libraries.description: Full-featured React component library with 100+ components, hooks, dark mode, and excellent developer experience.
libraries.when_to_use: When you need batteries-included development with 100+ polished components. Great for teams wanting speed + consistency with minimal configuration.
libraries.example:
import { Button, Group } from '@mantine/core'

<Group>
  <Button variant="filled">Filled</Button>
  <Button variant="outline">Outline</Button>
</Group>

libraries.name: Chakra UI
libraries.description: Accessible, themeable component library with great DX. Approachable for teams wanting speed + consistency.
libraries.when_to_use: When you want an approachable, well-documented component library with good defaults. Great for teams and rapid development.
libraries.example:
import { Button, Stack } from '@chakra-ui/react'

<Stack direction="row">
  <Button colorScheme="blue">Primary</Button>
  <Button variant="outline">Secondary</Button>
</Stack>

libraries.name: NextUI
libraries.description: Beautiful, fast, modern React UI library with great defaults. Built on Tailwind CSS.
libraries.when_to_use: When you need an app that looks polished immediately with minimal effort. Great for landing pages, MVPs, and Tailwind projects.
libraries.example:
import { Button } from '@nextui-org/react'

<Button color="primary" variant="shadow">Click me</Button>

libraries.name: React Bits
libraries.description: 110+ animated, creative React components for standout UIs. Text animations, backgrounds, cursors, cards, and more.
libraries.when_to_use: When you need creative, animated UI effects for landing pages, portfolios, or anywhere you want visual impact. Great for hero sections, backgrounds, and interactive elements.
libraries.example:
import { SplitText } from 'react-bits-ui'

<SplitText text="Hello World" className="text-4xl font-bold" delay={50} />

libraries.name: Tailwind CSS
libraries.description: Utility-first CSS framework. The industry standard for custom design systems and rapid UI development.
libraries.when_to_use: Default styling choice for modern React projects. Use when you want full design control with rapid iteration. Pairs with every component library.
libraries.example: Title

libraries.name: CSS Modules / Vanilla Extract
libraries.description: Zero-runtime typed CSS solutions. Scoped styles with no JavaScript runtime cost.
libraries.when_to_use: When performance matters and you want typed, scoped CSS with zero runtime overhead. Great for large-scale apps where CSS-in-JS bundle size is a concern.

libraries.name: Panda CSS
libraries.description: CSS-in-JS with a design token mindset. Build-time CSS generation with type safety.
libraries.when_to_use: When building a design system with strict token governance. Excellent for teams that want CSS-in-JS DX with zero-runtime output.
libraries.example:
import { css } from '../styled-system/css'
Styled with Panda

libraries.name: Framer Motion
libraries.description: The go-to animation library for React. Declarative animations, gestures, layout transitions, and spring physics.
libraries.when_to_use: Default animation library for React. Use for any animation: page transitions, hover effects, modals, lists, drag interactions, scroll animations.

libraries.name: GSAP
libraries.description: The most powerful animation library. Framework-agnostic, complex timelines, ScrollTrigger, and premium plugins.
libraries.when_to_use: When you need complex, sequenced animations or scroll-driven effects that go beyond Framer Motion. Essential for creative/agency websites.
libraries.example:
import gsap from 'gsap'
import { ScrollTrigger } from 'gsap/ScrollTrigger'

gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger)

gsap.to('.element', {
  scrollTrigger: '.element',
  x: 100,
  duration: 1,
  ease: 'power2.out'
})

libraries.name: React Spring
libraries.description: Physics-based animations for React. Natural motion with spring dynamics.
libraries.when_to_use: When you want physics-based animations that feel natural. Alternative to Framer Motion with a different API style.

libraries.name: Three.js / React Three Fiber
libraries.description: The foundational WebGL library for 3D in the browser. React Three Fiber makes it declarative JSX.
features: Drei helpers (cameras, lights, shaders, HTML overlays)
libraries.when_to_use: When you need 3D in the browser. Product viewers, interactive backgrounds, data visualization in 3D, immersive experiences.
libraries.example:
import { Canvas } from '@react-three/fiber'
import { OrbitControls, Sphere } from '@react-three/drei'

<Canvas>
  <ambientLight />
  <Sphere args={[1, 64, 64]}>
    <meshStandardMaterial color="hotpink" />
  </Sphere>
  <OrbitControls />
</Canvas>

libraries.name: Lottie
libraries.description: Render After Effects animations in the browser. Lightweight, scalable vector animations.
libraries.when_to_use: When you have designer-created animations from After Effects or want rich animated icons/illustrations without complex code.
libraries.example:
import Lottie from 'lottie-react'
import animationData from './animation.json'

<Lottie animationData={animationData} loop={true} />

libraries.name: tsParticles
libraries.description: Highly customizable particle effects for backgrounds and interactive elements.
libraries.when_to_use: When you need particle effects for backgrounds, celebrations (confetti), or ambient visual effects.
libraries.example:
import Particles from '@tsparticles/react'

<Particles options={{ particles: { number: { value: 50 }, move: { enable: true } } }} />

libraries.name: D3.js
libraries.description: The most powerful data visualization library. Low-level, bindable, infinite flexibility.
libraries.when_to_use: When you need custom, complex data visualizations that go beyond standard chart libraries. Maps, force-directed graphs, custom chart types.
libraries.example:
import * as d3 from 'd3'

d3.select('svg')
  .selectAll('rect')
  .data(data)
  .join('rect')
  .attr('height', d => yScale(d.value))

libraries.name: Recharts
libraries.description: Composable chart components for React. Built on D3, easy to use.
libraries.when_to_use: Default charting library for React dashboards and analytics UIs. When you need standard chart types with minimal configuration.
libraries.example:
import { LineChart, Line, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip } from 'recharts'

<LineChart data={data}>
  <CartesianGrid strokeDasharray="3 3" />
  <XAxis dataKey="name" />
  <YAxis />
  <Tooltip />
  <Line type="monotone" dataKey="value" stroke="#8884d8" />
</LineChart>

libraries.name: Victory
libraries.description: Composable React charting library with great animation support.
libraries.when_to_use: When you need animated charts or cross-platform charting (React + React Native). Alternative to Recharts with better animation.
libraries.example:
import { VictoryBar, VictoryChart } from 'victory'

<VictoryChart>
  <VictoryBar data={data} x="label" y="value" />
</VictoryChart>

premium_stack.description: Recommended premium UI stack for modern React apps
premium_stack.note: Tailwind + Shadcn + Framer Motion + React Three Fiber/Drei for anything with 3D = a very capable, modern setup.
